payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yes. Also, as a heads up because I saw some of you guys ask me in the chatroom: These videos will be available on the Geek & Sundry YouTube Channel soon enough, as well as a more extensive backstory of the party. We'll have all that content ready for you so you don't have to memorize it. Maybe a bio page, I don't know. We'll figure something out. It'll be fine. Anyway, let's open that up. You know who I am. Matthew Mercer, voice actor and I've been running this game for two years. I'm a hardcore nerd. Let's go around the table and introduce our players so you can attach the faces to the intros you just saw. Let's start on this side with Travis.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yes! 826LA is a charity that focuses on tutoring children in an after-school program and helping specifically with creativity, storytelling, art and general things that relate to Dungeons & Dragons. You should definitely check out the Time Travel Mart. There is one in Echo Park and one in Mar Vista. They have this amazing storefront where you can buy these awesome tchotchkes and things that go immediately to support the charity and the stream. It's awesome. It's 826LA, you should look it up. There's a few all over the country.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right! Let's jump on in. Thank you. Last we left off, to give you a little backstory. The party had completed a large venture in saving the nearby city of Emon. One of the central capitals of this human civilization of Tal'Dorei. They managed to halt a demonic insurrection within the throne and as such were greeted to a hero's celebration and had a keep built in their honor. Over the six month period of the keep being constructed, they went their own ways and then returned to see its final creation. However, they did not have a chance to really enjoy it immediately as one of their good friends and allies, Arcanist Allura Vysoren of the Tal'Dorei council came to them requesting their aid saying that a long time friend of hers, Lady Kima of Vord, who is a very well known and very well respected halfling paladin of Bahamut, the Platinum Dragon, had been gone on a pilgrimage for a while, essentially a vision quest that is part of her own development as a paladin. As part of this, she let the information go to Allura that a dark vision had come to her saying that some sort of evil root is beginning to breed beneath Kraghammer and the mountains within Kraghammer, which is the nearby dwarven civilization that you guys have previously not been allowed entry to because the dwarves weren't fans of nobody without any political means of entry. However, she managed to acquire the necessary documentation and offered you a very substantial reward should you find the whereabouts of Lady Kima of Vord and hopefully bring her back safely. You left on the pathway to the dwarven citadel of Kraghammer. You were ambushed by a group of roaming barbarian goliaths. Part way through the battle, Grog managed to recognize one of them as a previous ally and no longer an ally at this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I am Keyleth, the druid from the tribe of the Air Ashari. I am a half-elven person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, you're elven.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, half-elf.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Half-elf. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Come on, man. I've been half-elf since day one. Half-elven. Yes! You can see me shooting lightning and turning into awesome animals.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Orion.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hi.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Hey, everybody. I'm Orion Acaba and I'm also a voice actor. I do things.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm Marisha Ray, by the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did I say that?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth is Marisha Ray.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let Orion have his moment. Don't try and break--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: No, it's our moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm Tiberius the awesome sorcerer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Dragonborn sorcerer.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Dragonborn, yeah! That's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm Sam Riegel  a voice actor, and stuff. My character is Scanlan Shorthalt. The gnome bard who sings a lot. Let's start playing, yeah? (cheering)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Giles!" One of the dwarf barmaiders comes over. "Aye?" "Get Stepheson out of his room, move him to the third floor. We have to open a block for our new guests." "Ugh, fine." The older dwarf stumbles his way up the stairs begrudgingly. "Right, so I can get a room for each of you. We're looking at eight rooms separately, that will run you per evening about 25 gold pieces. Does that sound about right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What was her name?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Excuse me, miss. You.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Adra. Yeah?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Adra."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Adra! You run a fantastic establishment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's right I do!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for welcoming us and allowing us to buy this round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course. Patrons that come and drop coin like that are welcome any time."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, thank you for your hospitality. Might we inquire about staying here the night? Do you have an inn with the establishment?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She reaches below and pulls out a big tome and starts thumbing through it. "You know, actually we have openings right now on the second floor."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Several rooms, or just one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Do you need them side by side?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That would be nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We could pull him there and push him into room seven."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Something with a little bed for me.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Right! Well how bout we pay the week in advance and I'll give you a discount?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Total?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "25 total pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You want to share a room?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! We'll bunk up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you and me. I can sleep on your feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Probably like 15 gold then for just that amount.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She visibly deflates a little bit and is like, "All right, fair enough. Five rooms it is. 15 gold per room."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're penny pinchers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How long do you expect to stay?"

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: At least a few days.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'd say a week.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: With an indefinite hold, we have business in the city.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Oh, she went through a few weeks. I haven't seen her recently."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That shouldn't be a problem. Mostly we are looking for Lord Greyspine, if I recall. We have business with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We might have business with you, if you've seen a halfling come through.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She thinks to herself and goes, "There was a halfling woman, but she was staying at the Firebrook Inn."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The Firebrook.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The bottom floor, for a couple of weeks. There was talk about her. She got in a few fights herself. The one with the silver armor? That one? Yeah, she was a firebrand if ever I saw one. Right up my alley, I like that one."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How long ago would you say this was?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Oh, well there's probably several at this point, I've lost track. The family line's been going for a while. However, of House Greyspine currently, Ironkeeper Gradim Greyspine is the head of this entire city. Voted in ten years ago, actually. He's head of the council."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: My type of dwarf. Maybe we'll can use him as a merc later or something. All right, another time then.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Another time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Save it for a rainy day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Does it rain down here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't think they have rainy days.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You said Greyspine?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, Greyspine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We are looking to set up a meeting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Which Greyspine?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Nostoc?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Nostoc Greyspine. He's definitely a business type one."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Well who's the friendlier Greypsine?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The friendlier of the types would probably be the head of House Greyspine."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, how many Greypsines are there?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He pours another drink. At which point, Adra slaps his hand and says, "Pay that. He bought one round!" He finishes his drink and walks away.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Down at the quarry?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Aye."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How did your friend get this nasty cut?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He works there! Said there was things pouring out of the caves. Goblins and the like!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Goblins?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ah, goblins are nothing."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Seems there's something other than goblins poking out through this hole. Just saying, that business is in for a serious problem if it doesn't change."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does your friend ever have drinks here? "No. He's been sober for two years. We're not friends anymore." Tugs on his beard a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I'm sorry. I think.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A lot to process.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Best that you can tell, inside-- you can't. You just arrived, probably around 3:00 or 4:00 in the afternoon, so you're probably pushing sunset right about now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So many racist comments. Jeez!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "What you're looking for is House Thunderbrand. They are the ones who, by blood, have the arcane arts in their family line."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Interesting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They run most of the enchantment process in the city, they also train-- they call it train, some of us call it more 'indoctrination.' But, they're definitely the focus of all arcane arts here in Kraghammer."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Thank you for your time, Adra. Carry on with your evening. We'll seek you out if we have any more questions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Certainly. Now if you don't mind," She scoots you aside, and you see a line, a queue of dwarves behind her, with their empty cups, waiting to get to her to fill up the drinks that you bought.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that's-- we did promise that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Enjoy, gentlemen! And lady.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What time of day is it?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "You've got five minutes." Then he stretches his back a bit. This gnarled hunch that he holds in his physical posture, flexes it a bit, and now you can see the rippling muscles that show beneath his tunic. This is a built dwarf. You don't know what past he's had, moving boulders or forging crazy iron shit, but there's a reason this guy has a reputation. He stands up off of his stool, which you can now hear crack with the weight of a dwarf, which are made for normal dwarves. This guy is solid muscle. He saunters over, looks about for a second, almost like he's looking for his next target. Sees the pointy ears, sees the pointy ears, and goes (sighs) and saunters up to the table, slams his hand down.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lost cause. Abandon ship!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right. I have an intelligence of six, I know what I'm doing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There is a very pretty lady over there, with slightly pointy ears. (whispering) I'm talking about you or you. (normal volume) There's two of them! They were actually wanting to ask you a question if you wouldn't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a persuasion roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Me?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why is he doing this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why would you throw this to us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I know!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Because I was either going to fight him or I was going to throw boobs at him, and I went with boobs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He realized he was sinking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's level ten you get that, by the way. Throw Boobs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Persuasion. Oh! Good roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Look at that  one. 19!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Woo!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 19? He takes a moment, and grabs the drink that you prepared from his hand and goes (gulps).

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Nice form.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I can give you the finest of thistle branch, dark blood wine. This was crafted by myself, from a rare bloodthistle branch that I had brought over from the far-off city of Kamordah. This, I personally oversaw. It took me four years to fill this barrel full. It'll cost you 500 gold pieces. But it is a king's red wine!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So you roll twice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What was the first thing you rolled?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Three. I'll take the 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Definitely take that 18.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh and plus my--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Persuasion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks at you keenly, which--

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Which is a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa, you have a nine persuasion?!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: That's why I talk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's pretty charismatic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "Well no, I don't really want to go anywhere with you. But if you're looking to go ahead and talk to the Greyspines, you don't want to go empty-handed. It's customary to come with a gift of some kind."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh really?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What might they like?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, let's just say, I have the finest brewery in the city."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I have some fine, very rare, very exotic drinks that I could, perchance, sell to you."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pay the dwarf.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much for this exotic ale?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How exotic you wanting?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We want to get in the door. We want him to like us.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Make it quick! You've got one minute."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Pay the dwarf.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How much for this exotic ale?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "How exotic you wanting?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We want to get in the door. We want him to like us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can give you the finest of thistle branch, dark blood wine. This was crafted by myself, from a rare bloodthistle branch that I had brought over from the far-off city of Kamordah. This, I personally oversaw. It took me four years to fill this barrel full. It'll cost you 500 gold pieces. But it is a king's red wine!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I do a persuasion check-- a perception check to see if he's lying?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would be insight. Go ahead and roll insight.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go walk over to Grog really quickly.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He seems too drunk to lie.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's just do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Tell you what, Balgus. We have some very rare items with us that I think you might be interested in.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The barrel is too big, you barely got Dork the ox in there. Which, for the record, they did fit a small ox in the bag of holding and then realized later on that there was no airflow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Top shelf at BevMo!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He pats it and he goes, "All yours. If this doesn't get you in the door, I'll eat my shoe."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Have you ever done that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Maybe, I forget a lot of nights."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Where might that door be?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Minute's up, you'll have to ask around, but it was great doing business with you." He walks away from the table.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I put it in the bag of holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It does not fit in the bag of holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What?!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You've technically been short resting in the tavern. It's more of like a post battle-- like, take a breather, bandage your wounds, settle yourself after a harrowing experience. That's what a short rest is.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: You should give us all a copy so you will have multiple.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I only have one."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay, that's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Sorry!"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Confidence needs improvement.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it's okay, we have a wonderful writer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'll rustle up a few. That's fine, why not. It's what I do, apparently.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He runs back into the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, shall we go to the manor?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do we want to do the manor right now? What time is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We got to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We might not want to bug him when he's, you know, having dinner.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's be honest, that gate's going to jack us up. It's going to be full of trouble.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, yes, let's go sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's only 6 00.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, then we have time to do other things.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: How long is a short rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Short rest is around ten minutes or so.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I want to take short rest.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: We keep short rest to ten minutes because it makes it move faster. Pike is going along, she seems to be a little distracted, she can't quite understand. She says, "I don't know, I feel like I've been here before. It's hard to explain."

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Sorry! I'm going to House Thunderbrand before turning in this evening. I've had a nice little short rest and I would like to ask them a few questions.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, go ahead, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is there any recon that's going to happen tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I think that you and I are going to go to Greyspine?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll check out the house.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you need any help?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, you can come with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I don't want you to say yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Then no, we'll stealth our way.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just a moment. Pike, you seem very distant. It's very strange, are you all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pike's not listening, she's looking at the chat room.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's feeling ill. Does she need to lie down?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike is feeling, and for those of you who are curious in the chat room, once again, elements of this are house ruled, so don't complain.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, players.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. You choose your lady, you take the tallest one which would probably be the elf. As you walk in, the shy elf turns around entirely. She is aggressive and throws you around. You got your money's worth. You get manhandled in a way that you haven't-- You're going to be sore in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Stone's Pillow?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Stone's Pillow.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I love it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's an establishment of comfort and ease, and will run you both for the evening, as you are not Kraghammer locals, 30 gold for the night each.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A bargain.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 30 gold?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What sort of ladies will we find there?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Bearded ladies.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What species?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The madam of the house introduces you to a spectrum of ladies, mostly dwarven, and very attractive, you know. This is a nice establishment. There is one gnome. One female gnome. There are a number of male dwarves as well, that are standing at the ready. There is one elf, looks a little shy and embarrassed that a goliath walked in.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And that's our choice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Those are your choices.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take the tallest one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll take a dwarf.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You ask around, and it takes a while, especially since as a dragonborn some folks are like, "Oh, I'm not talking to you!" Some others are like, "Oh, yeah, right over that way!" It's a completely wrong direction, so it takes you a good two hours to eventually find your way to House Thunderbrand. When you get there, it's a beautiful alabaster building: large, white dome, with small spires built in to one of the more elevated ceilings of Kraghammer proper. On the outside, there is no gate, just an open lawn. You see grass, you see normally outside flora and fauna currently adorning the scape around this structure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No! They're professional Carvers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: -- Vax, you vanish. Vex, you take a couple steps. You hear, "Hey, hey, hey! I told you, it's dangerous down there in the dark! Don't do that!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Of course!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Where's your friend?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he already went back to the tavern that we're staying at. I was wondering if maybe you could show me around the rest of the town?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks to the other guards and they're obviously fed up with his flirting, and they're not too keen on you guys. "Well, you guys hold up post, I'll give her a quick walk around."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, but I'm going to tell the boss!" "Fine! I don't get this chance very often!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I wave off from the alley that I snuck in to.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, go do your shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You do your shit, that you do! Then I head back through the shadows.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoosh! Damn it!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, as you run into the ring a couple of the dwarves pat you on the back, you get a shove into the ring. It's now Trinket and you--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll be right back. I head downstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You head downstairs and you can see all the dwarves are now gathered around the fighting ring in the center of the tavern.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket's now inside the ring with a couple other dwarves around, prodding and pushing it into the ring.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, no! No! No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's raucous cheering. They're not even listening to you at this point.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Grog!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I followed my sister downstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I run down to with Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, as you guys head down, you can see now Balgus is now up and rubbing his chin. "Oh, never fought a bear before! (laughs)"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, Balgus! Fuck! Trinket, come here! Are we allowed to cuss? Trinket, come here!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are very much allowed to cuss.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Isn't that right, guys? Fuck yeah!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, no, and I run into the ring with him.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the bar
payload.raw_text: You guys are pulling through a crowd, covered in mist still. You're having a hard time finding your way through the bar. The people are at least now cheering, in the mood. There's still some people trying to find their way to the front to see what's going because they hear the music and they hear the cheering. "Well, what's going on over there?" They're pushing their way through. You make your way to the bar. You see Adra's really nervous, like, (panicked sounds)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: This array of strange puffy fireworks in the fog; you doing this little rotation. You actually manage to do a bear cartwheel, and slam into the ring, but it looks pretty cool. You get back up. You're on your hind legs, you dance on one foot for a second. Balgus is like, (raucous laughter). He starts laughing, doubles over, clutching his stomach, laughing his ass off. Other dwarves start laughing too. The music gets louder and the band in the back of the tavern begins playing again. There's now this general jovial air restored to the tavern.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I grab an empty cup off of the table and say: Tips for the bear, right here!

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: We're going to get money for this?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Of course.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I beeline to the bar and say  A large mug of ale, please. As soon as it's ready, bring it right over to Balgus.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Yeah, there's a happy growl! Oh, it's been so long since I've been outside!" A little tear on the edge of his face. As you approach up his side--

preceding_turns.text: (growls)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Immediately, instinctively, he slaps it off his arm and turns around.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Take this! You are an impressive specimen, my good man. I have never had a fight that amazing in at least a week. That was something to see. Here, take this, and after this one there's three more.

payload.raw_text: Anything you guys want to do in preparation for the next day?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh, so he's fine.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Half of it's filled with barrels and crates, and storage of dried foods. Trinket comes in and (sniffing) and starts rummaging into one of the half open sacks of dried meat. That's going to be put on your bill.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Shit.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You head back to your rooms for the evening to rest.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Take a nap.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Anything you guys want to do in preparation for the next day?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he's a bear, he's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (as Trinket) Yeah, I'm fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, yeah, he's seems to think it's all right. Let's go with the storage room. If there's anything that looks edible, you can eat it. It's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Trinket becomes a bear again. I'd say the room's probably a good 40 feet by 30 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Half of it's filled with barrels and crates, and storage of dried foods. Trinket comes in and (sniffing) and starts rummaging into one of the half open sacks of dried meat. That's going to be put on your bill.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

payload.raw_text: Hello, everyone. Welcome back. We've had our pee breaks. We've had our refills of our drinks. We've got Fireball, so I think we're good for the next venture. Anyway, the party has taken their rest for the evening. Once again, this is one of those weird experiences where you wake up, not because of the sun rising, because you can't see it in the dwarven city of Kraghammer, but eventually you all come to consciousness naturally. What is your plan of action?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys make your way to the residential district. The same guards are there who were there the previous night that encountered you guys so, as you roll up with an entourage-

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's where the troll dick came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Don't you remember, Vax? I was almost raped by trolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There was a cavern interaction with two male trolls that they had stealthed up behind as a party. I love explaining this to the audience. Tiberius then polymorphed into a female troll to try and Bugs Bunny them, not realizing that dwarves who are underground and--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Trolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: --trolls don't encounter female trolls often enough to have a one track mind. He nearly got himself in a very bad situation. That was remedied by the party attacking and Vex over here shooting off the troll's dick. Which then went in the bag of holding, which is disgusting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's also where the hashtag not all trolls came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yup, hashtag not all trolls came from that game. That is horrible.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh god. Bring it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Bringing it back.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the very
payload.raw_text: They all look about each other. "Fine. Remember, we're waiting." They all lick their lips a little. They part and let you guys into the residential district. You make your way to the very back. You can see House Greyspine. Once again, guys, for those who haven't been there before, it's a beautiful arrangement. The iron gate around it with spikes on the top. You can see the entire front yard, it's about a good 80 yard walk to the front of the building itself. It's a quite a distance from the front gate and that entire area is filled with like a stone garden with various small sculptures and interesting knick knacks they collected and put on display like as a small museum as you approach the front of House Greyspine. You get to the front door as a large double oak door about ten feet tall, four feet wide, currently closed. There is no handle on the door.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, keep nodding. Here's the thing. I don't know if I can save four bottles worth, but I'll save as much as I can.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Just say yes.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Hey, I've got it covered.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: On our father's good name, we will bring you some of this. That is a promise.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's a good name.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Because you're invisible, I'll give you advantage on that. Go ahead and roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I use the earpiece and whisper  What are you doing, Scanlan?! What are you doing?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know! Give me 20 minutes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 20 minutes, that's okay! Let's all step away from the house.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ask if he needs assistance or divergent--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Divergent?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Divergent.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Magrim wipes his hands and walks away going, "(sigh) Fucking foreigners."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I follow him where he's going.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My nervousness, I let out a little bit of a fart as we--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a stealth check.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Why are you making it harder on yourself?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 14 plus I'm invisible, plus two. 16.

payload.trigger_phrase: You make your way to the edge
payload.raw_text: Yes, the bard was doing a lockpick with no lockpicks. He was fashioning it from wire. Exciting but pointless! You make your way to the edge of the center ring. You find that there are long spiral staircases that move to the bottom, where there are elevators that bring you to the bottom floor. The elevators are chain based, like a ratchet-type system with two Carvers there, of course. You approach one, eventually you talk with them, they lead you down there, but once again they give you a look of, "Watch your back!"

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: She's feeling awfully BAFTA today, so.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Let's make our way to the mines, shall we?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns_last5.text: Metal poisoning, yes.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: To the mines, then, yeah.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Let's go get this drunk dwarf!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: They nod, and let you pass. As you continue downward into the lower area, you can see the red stone that lets a lot of the atmospheric glow of Kraghammer is used less and less and a lot more central torches are now in place; sconces. The stone itself is either rarer the further down you go or has less of an illuminating power the deeper into the earth. You eventually reach into the center part of town. You now see from where you're standing a number of large structures in the very center of the bottom part of the city of Kraghammer. You can see-- There is a giant domed temple that is made of gold and bronze in the center. It looks almost like a gargantuan gazebo that has doorways on each side that is currently closed off. No guards at it, though. You do see a number of other buildings. There is a large, heavy foundry far off to the left of you that has a little bit of smoke that's coming off of it, but most of it looks to be filtered into a giant, metallic funnel that is then placed into the rock. Almost like all the smoke based exhaust is being funneled outside of the mountain. That is the closest; that's the foundry. Then to the right of you, you wander a bit and you can begin to see that part of the mountainside to the far right has that look of a strip mine, or like a large portion of the minerals has been pulled away and is currently being mined further. That looks like probably one of the better places to look in looking for the Greyspine Quarry. You eventually make your way to the outside of the quarry. You now look down into three large pits that descend deeply into the side of the mountain. This mine has been going for quite some time and a large chunk of this looks like it may be being prepped; almost like the mine is what's growing Kraghammer. The more they mine into the mountain, the more they fill the mined area with further city construction. It's this self perpetuating cycle of expanding the city downward. Currently in the quarry, there are a number of large stone dwarven buildings in the edge of it, one of which is the largest of them all, looks like it's made more of a black and red, iron aesthetic; some of it's been oxidized a little bit and has that rough and tumble, iron feel. That one has the most guards around the base of it. The Carvers are gathered entirely around it. Looking down into the center of the quarry, towards the bottom there are a number of tunnels that lead into the hillside of Cliffkeep Mountains proper, surrounding Kraghammer.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She wanted to stay at the inn this morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She's feeling awfully BAFTA today, so.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Everyone roll initiative. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There is an open funnel towards the tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to throw a little Fireball, right there where those guys are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you guys you all hit from this distance because goblins are puny little things. They're still a little small to see, but you're all pretty well trained and pretty honed with your aim. You fire simultaneously, gunshot echo through the hallway, arrows, flying dagger, you see three goblins fall off a nearby dwarf who goes, "Thank--" At which point a large fireball explodes, killing off about ten or so of the goblins and probably two of the dwarves.

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Sorry about that!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The guards stopped.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They they start looking around and they see you pull out your weapons and they all take a step back and realize they don't want to die either. "Well go, if you're going to help, go!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We run straight in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With you guys charging in, you can now see a handful more goblins are rushing out. They are like running really fast, sprinting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I throw Entangle up, in the distance wherever they're coming from.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, what's the radius on that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like a hundred feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's the range. What is the radius?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, 20-foot radius.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You find a portion of the central point where the things exit from the tunnel and these strange reddish vines begin to protrude and to begin to grab nearby goblins and pull them up in this massive, weird vine creature. You hear (stomping) and you now see two larger ogre-like creatures come storming out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: ORION
preceding_turns.text: Wonderful!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 17. Okay. The ogre that's been beaten, blasted by the fireball, it's getting up-- (grunt). It grabs its club, and it begins to get up on one leg as the Lightning Arrow slams into its chest. You can see it hits. Looks down for a second, and then the charge pulses through its entire torso. As it does, it screams out, "Ugh!" into the cavern, its whole muscle system locks tight and it falls forward again, onto its chest, smoke rising off of its whole upper area, unmoving, on the ground. He is gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Ladies first.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Well, all right. I'm going to cast Hunter's Mark on the most damaged one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that'd be this one here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right. Then I'm going to shoot Lightning Arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Do you want to move a little closer?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I do.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That would put you right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, cool. I'm going to shoot Lightning Arrow at them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You pull out your arrow and as you pull it back, a little bit of energy courses through your fingers, strikes through your arrow. You can now see this crackling energy as you're pulling it taut in your bow. You let it loose, roll for attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay! 21!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 21 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right. Then I do something else. 1d8. Right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep. You do the damage of the arrow plus the damage of the spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Holy moly! So that's this. 13 for the arrow and then 4d8 for the spell. (counting) 17!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You just ultra killed him! (valley girl voice) He is so dead. Oh my god. This ogre is done. (normal voice) All right. The other ogre's angry. It's now the goblins' turn. These goblins rush up. They are dashing forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Dang! Come on now!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Then the other one takes 2d8 if he fails his saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Which, with a one, he does.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes! Oh, he fails it so good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead a roll damage for other ogre.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Two. ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten damage. Nice. As he falls to the ground, the arc arcs over to the other one who drops his club for a second. Picks it up angrily.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can reassign my Hunter's Mark to him now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, all right. That brings us to--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, my Hunter's Mark! I didn't do my Hunter's Mark damage. But it doesn't matter. He's dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't matter. You killed him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, cool.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "We don't know! It's been different things. Strange abominations. It's hard to describe. They're put together. Something's making things down there."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are there any guards around still? Any dwarven guards?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They are all watching you do this now. They don't want to put their lives at risk, and you guys seem intent on getting into the middle of this battle, so they are going to sit back and let you do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I throw up my wall and turn around and I say: What's coming?! Tell us! You know what's coming. What's coming?!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an intimidation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, you are so hardcore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm not good at this.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You speak goblin?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, four. (laughter and groaning)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (tearfully) What's coming?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Make a pity roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: A very gentle attempt at intimidation, however, the message gets across. The guards look at you, taken off guard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We are about to die. It is in your best interest to tell us.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You've had a moment to train. You haven't seen the creature until it just burst out, so it would take you a round to aim.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do naga heads do something if you look at them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We're beginning back at the top of the initiative order.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait! I got a question! My Hunter's Mark, can I transfer it to that thing or do I have to--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, you can, yes. Because this still part of the encounter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sweet!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is now marked with the Hunter's Mark. Percy, you're up first.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Do I have to take new aim or can I have myself already trained on--
